verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24 family that cycles between ER and Golgi, functioning in COPI/COPII vesicle cargo selection and ER-Golgi trafficking. Its steady-state localization is ER-Golgi endomembrane. Not cell-state-induced to PM (no stress/oncogenic surface display documented); not tissue-restricted surface; not lysosomal exocytosis substrate; no documented stable PM pool (cycling is intra-endomembrane, not PM); not a wash-resistant attachment to a PM partner. No antibody/ADC/CAR programs target it on the cell surface. Recent work on TMED10-channeled misfolded protein secretion concerns ERGIC translocation, not surface display of TMED10 itself.
